CONTENT.Career: In June 2019, it was announced Manu would join 2. Bundesliga side SV Darmstadt 98 from 3. Liga club Hallescher FC for the 2019–20 season having agreed a contract until 2022.Manu re-joined Hallescher FC on loan for the rest of the 2020–21 season in January 2021.On 12 May 2024, Darmstadt 98 announced that he and several other players will leave the club after this season.On 30 July 2024, Manu signed a two-year contract with an option for a third year with PEC Zwolle in the Netherlands.
CONTENT.Personal life: Born in Germany, Manu is of Ghanaian descent.In September 2021, Manu was called up to the Ghana national team for a World Cup qualifier against South Africa. He remained on the bench in the game.
CONTENT.External links: Braydon Manu at WorldFootball.netBraydon Manu at Soccerway

SUMMARY: Braydon Marvin Manu (born 28 March 1997) is a professional footballer who plays as a forward or right winger for Dutch Eredivisie club PEC Zwolle. Born in Germany, he represents Ghana internationally.
